intro: Callicarpa tzitanshaniana, also known as the Tibetan Purple Mint, is a rare and unique plant species native to the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and protection against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Callicarpa tzitanshaniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Tibetan medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea to treat various ailments, making it a valuable resource for herbal practitioners seeking natural remedies.
